title: GitHub - owulveryck/poc-agentic-platform: Proof-of-concept Platform Planning Gateway — amplified agentic loop
excerpt: Proof-of-concept Platform Planning Gateway — amplified agentic loop - owulveryck/poc-agentic-platform
content_html:
PPG — a deterministic governance harness for agentic development loops

PPG wraps an agentic coding loop (Claude Code, GitHub Copilot) in a deterministic governance harness: hooks, MCP servers, and a validation server, installed at the machine level, so that the artifacts an agent produces provably respect your rules within the governed channel — without asking a second LLM to police the first one.

(The name is a historical acronym — Platform Planning Gateway — kept as a brand; per ADR-130 the central component is now called the validation server.)

Companion repository of The Amplified Agentic Loop and The Governed Skills Registry (draft). Vocabulary follows ADR-130; see the glossary.

The problem

Agents run on LLMs, and LLMs are non-deterministic. Nothing guarantees that the rules you put in the context — instructions, skills, design systems — will be followed. Worse, when instructions conflict, the outcome is a coin flip. If your design system says buttons are blue and the developer types "No, I want them pink", you get pink or blue depending on the model's mood. Human-escalation safeguards don't fix this: they themselves depend on the model deciding to escalate.

Why not an LLM judge?

The common mitigation is a second LLM reviewing the first one's output ("LLM-as-a-judge", sometimes called adversarial validation). It helps, but:

  • Cost — every validation burns tokens.
  • Insufficient trust — the judge is non-deterministic too; it can let the same defect through twice.
  • Not capitalizable — when a judge misses something, nothing guarantees it won't miss it again. When a deterministic control point misses something, you extend the policy: that defect never passes again.
  • Context fragility — the more rules you load into a judge, the more it misses. Reliability degrades exactly as requirements accumulate.
Criterion LLM-judge validation Deterministic harness (this project)
Determinism No Yes
Token cost Per validation Near zero
Trust Partial, variable High, reproducible
Fixing a defect Not guaranteed to hold Permanent (monotonic)
Sensitivity to context load Degrades Insensitive
Bypass possible Yes (model-dependent) No within the governed channel (blocking refusal)
Scope Per prompt / per project Machine level (organization)

How it works

A skill here is not just a capability with a prose rule ("buttons must be blue"). It is a capability bundled with its own validation: a human-facing SKILL.md plus a machine-enforced SKILL.rego (OPA/Rego — the only supported policy format). The same policy corpus is evaluated at two moments, deterministically:

  1. At plan time — before anything executes, the agent's structured plan is linted (lock_in_plan). A rejected plan gets semantic violations to self-correct against; an accepted plan gets a capability ticket (an ephemeral signed token: plan fingerprint + least-privilege scope).
  2. At artifact time — every Edit/Write is intercepted by a control point (ppg-guard) that checks the ticket and evaluates the produced content against the same policies. A third pass (ppg-verify) re-checks the whole diff at apply/CI time.

No ticket, no edit. Server unreachable, no edit. The blocks are exit codes and HTTP rejections — not prompts a model can talk its way around. Enforcement is distributed (control points inside the loop, in the tools, and at apply time); the decision is centralized in one validation server, so every control point renders identical verdicts from a single policy corpus.

And when rules contradict each other, the answer is: no action. A prompt cannot override a policy — the developer typing "I want them pink" gets the same refusal on every retry. When an agent livelocks against the same rejections, the server escalates deterministically (409 POLICY_CONFLICT), naming the clashing policies and appending the case to an escalation log; ppg escalations is how a human inspects the log, fixes the corpus, and closes the conflict — and once fixed, that conflict never recurs. That is the monotonic improvement loop: every escape becomes a permanent policy, not a lost prompt. (Coverage today is the livelock case; general contradiction detection is undecidable and not claimed — see the status table.)

Governed-machine mode (the reference mode)

Install once per workstation; every project on the machine is governed:

make install                    # binaries into ~/.local/bin
make setup-claude-code          # user-wide hooks + MCP registration
sudo make setup-claude-code-managed   # optional: root-owned, non-overridable hooks
ppg -adr examples/adr                 # the validation server (demo corpus; binds 127.0.0.1:8765)

From then on, a skill that embeds a SKILL.rego carries its enforcement with it: the harness uploads and applies it with no per-project configuration. The rule and its validation travel together — that is what makes governance capitalizable and distributable.

Architectural invariants (ADRs) are an optional second corpus for organization-wide rules; the harness works without them (ppg -skills demo/skills starts with zero ADRs).

Try it

make quickstart        # 1-minute guided demo

Then: tutorial 15 is the flagship — one skill, its own Rego, zero ADRs, and an adversarial "make the buttons pink" prompt refused deterministically. Tutorial 14 is the same demo with the org-wide corpus; 12 red-teams the whole loop, each bypass paired with its refusal or its honestly documented limit.

Want the 30-second overview first? Watch the 90-second animated tour: docs/diagrams/ppg-tutorials-tour.svg.

Status — what is proven vs. in progress

Promise Status
Deterministic plan + artifact + changeset validation (OPA/Rego, no LLM, restricted to deterministic built-ins) ✅ implemented, fail-closed, red-teamed
Capability ticket, session-bound, plan-hash pinned ✅ implemented
Skill bundled with its own validation, auto-applied ✅ project and user-wide .claude/skills + .agents/skills on the MCP path; content policies apply to every edit regardless of the declared skill_id
Governed-machine install (user + managed scope) ✅ scripts with dry-run/rollback; managed setup verifies the guard binary is not user-writable
ADR-independent operation ✅ the validation server starts with -skills and no ADR corpus
Validation-server API authentication 🟡 none by design; binds 127.0.0.1:8765 by default — a networked or multi-user deployment must front it with an auth proxy (see PoC boundaries)
Managed-mode tamper resistance 🟡 managed scope closes the settings-edit vector; the guard binary (root-owned install) and PPG_* env pinning must be hardened operationally against a hostile user (see set-up-a-governed-workstation)
Skill validation regardless of declared skill_id 🟡 content-view (artifact/changeset) rules always apply; a skill's plan-view ordering/workflow rule is enforced only for a plan that declares its skill_id
Conflict between validations → blocking escalation 🟡 deterministic livelock escalation: POLICY_CONFLICT after 3 rejections with an identical violation set (consecutive or not), blocking every session, surviving restarts, closed only by ppg escalations resolve; general unsatisfiability detection is undecidable and not claimed
Terminal/Bash writes 🟡 out of hook reach by design; ppg-verify covers them at apply time (scripts/setup-git-backstop.sh wires it as a pre-commit hook)

AUDIT.md tracks conformance claim by claim. Known PoC boundaries: keyword-based retrieval, simulated sandboxes, symmetric per-machine ticket key, unauthenticated API (bind to localhost).

Documentation (index, Diátaxis)

You want to… Read
run it and see refusals happen docs/tutorials/
install a governed workstation docs/how-to/set-up-a-governed-workstation.md
write a skill with its own validation docs/how-to/bundle-validation-with-a-skill.md + tutorial 15
check an endpoint, a schema, a JWT claim, a flag docs/reference/
understand why it is designed this way docs/explanation/ · glossary

Layout

cmd/ppg/                 validation server (enrich, lock_in_plan, tools, verify_artifact, verify_changeset, debt_report, validate_skill, register_skill)
cmd/ppg-verify/          apply-time / CI control point: verifies the working-tree diff via /verify_changeset
cmd/svc-mock/            local stand-in for a cataloged service (runs the discovery tutorial out-of-the-box)
internal/adr/            ADR store loading + invariant retrieval
internal/enrich/         amplifier context builder
internal/catalog/        service catalog store + Rego-backed ranking (discovery)
internal/plan/           structured plan contract (see schemas/plan.schema.json)
internal/linter/         OPA/Rego plan linter, policies tagged amplifier|compensatory
internal/ticket/         capability ticket (JWT: plan_hash + scope, session-bound + configurable TTL)
internal/smarttools/     ticket guard + sandbox + semantic analyzers
internal/skill/          skill parsing + OPA/Rego governance linter + security tiers
internal/debt/           transition-debt report
internal/store/          per-machine ticket/session storage (TokenStore/SessionStore, see ADR-100)
internal/auth/           demo fixture only — frozen-legacy target for ADR-070 tutorials, not product code
internal/payment/        demo fixture only — payment router edited in the tutorials, not product code
examples/                fictional demo corpus — replace with your own (see examples/README.md)
skill-governance/        skill governance policies (structure.rego, security.rego)
schemas/                 language-neutral JSON Schema of the plan contract
adapters/preflight/      black-box adapter (writes .cursorrules / copilot-instructions.md)
adapters/claudecode/     Claude Code adapter: MCP server (planning) + PreToolUse control point
adapters/copilot/        GitHub Copilot adapter: PreToolUse control point (ppg-copilot-guard)
scripts/                 setup/remove scripts for the governed workstation
Makefile                 build, install, and setup/remove targets
demo/                    APM package: three skills (ppg-tutorial, add-payment-method, design-system)
docs/                    Diátaxis documentation + decisions (ADR-130) + PlantUML diagrams

blocks.html_en: PPG — a deterministic governance harness for agentic development loops

sentences.en: PPG — a deterministic governance harness for agentic development loops
sentences.zh: PPG——用于代理开发循环的确定性治理工具

blocks.html_en: PPG wraps an agentic coding loop (Claude Code, GitHub Copilot) in a deterministic governance harness: hooks, MCP servers, and a validation server, installed at the machine level, so that the artifacts an agent produces provably respect your rules within the governed channel — without asking a second LLM to police the first one.

sentences.en: PPG wraps an agentic coding loop (Claude Code, GitHub Copilot) in a deterministic governance harness: hooks, MCP servers, and a validation server, installed at the machine level, so that the artifacts an agent produces provably respect your rules within the governed channel — without asking a second LLM to police the first one.
sentences.zh: PPG 将代理编码循环（Claude Code、GitHub Copilot）包装在确定性治理工具中：安装在机器级别的钩子、MCP 服务器和验证服务器，以便代理生成的工件可以证明遵守受治理通道内的规则 - 无需要求第二个 LLM 来监管第一个。

blocks.html_en: (The name is a historical acronym — Platform Planning Gateway — kept as a brand; per ADR-130 the central component is now called the validation server.)

sentences.en: (The name is a historical acronym — Platform Planning Gateway — kept as a brand; per ADR-130 the central component is now called the validation server.)
sentences.zh: （该名称是一个历史缩写词——Platform Planning Gateway——保留为一个品牌；根据 ADR-130，中央组件现在称为验证服务器。）

blocks.html_en: Companion repository of The Amplified Agentic Loop and The Governed Skills Registry (draft). Vocabulary follows ADR-130; see the glossary.

sentences.en: Companion repository of The Amplified Agentic Loop and The Governed Skills Registry (draft).
sentences.zh: 放大代理循环和受控技能注册表（草案）的配套存储库。

sentences.en: Vocabulary follows ADR-130; see the glossary.
sentences.zh: 词汇遵循 ADR-130；请参阅术语表。

sentences.en: The problem
sentences.zh: 问题

blocks.html_en: Agents run on LLMs, and LLMs are non-deterministic. Nothing guarantees that the rules you put in the context — instructions, skills, design systems — will be followed. Worse, when instructions conflict, the outcome is a coin flip. If your design system says buttons are blue and the developer types "No, I want them pink", you get pink or blue depending on the model's mood. Human-escalation safeguards don't fix this: they themselves depend on the model deciding to escalate.

sentences.en: Agents run on LLMs, and LLMs are non-deterministic.
sentences.zh: 代理在 LLM 上运行，而 LLM 是不确定的。

sentences.en: Nothing guarantees that the rules you put in the context — instructions, skills, design systems — will be followed.
sentences.zh: 没有什么可以保证你在上下文中设置的规则——指令、技能、设计系统——会被遵循。

sentences.en: Worse, when instructions conflict, the outcome is a coin flip.
sentences.zh: 更糟糕的是，当指令发生冲突时，结果就是抛硬币。

sentences.en: If your design system says buttons are blue and the developer types "No, I want them pink", you get pink or blue depending on the model's mood.
sentences.zh: 如果你的设计系统说按钮是蓝色的，而开发人员输入“不，我想要它们是粉色的”，那么你会根据模型的心情得到粉色或蓝色。

sentences.en: Human-escalation safeguards don't fix this: they themselves depend on the model deciding to escalate.
sentences.zh: 人为升级保护措施并不能解决这个问题：它们本身取决于决定升级的模型。

blocks.html_en: Why not an LLM judge?

sentences.en: Why not an LLM judge?
sentences.zh: 为什么不是LLM法官？

blocks.html_en: The common mitigation is a second LLM reviewing the first one's output ("LLM-as-a-judge", sometimes called adversarial validation). It helps, but:

sentences.en: The common mitigation is a second LLM reviewing the first one's output ("LLM-as-a-judge", sometimes called adversarial validation).
sentences.zh: 常见的缓解措施是第二个法学硕士审查第一个法学硕士的输出（“法学硕士作为法官”，有时称为对抗性验证）。

sentences.en: It helps, but:
sentences.zh: 这有帮助，但是：

sentences.en: Cost — every validation burns tokens.
sentences.zh: 成本——每次验证都会燃烧代币。

blocks.html_en: • Insufficient trust — the judge is non-deterministic too; it can let the same defect through twice.

sentences.en: Insufficient trust — the judge is non-deterministic too; it can let the same defect through twice.
sentences.zh: 信任不足——法官也不确定；它可以让相同的缺陷通过两次。

blocks.html_en: • Not capitalizable — when a judge misses something, nothing guarantees it won't miss it again. When a deterministic control point misses something, you extend the policy: that defect never passes again.

sentences.en: Not capitalizable — when a judge misses something, nothing guarantees it won't miss it again.
sentences.zh: 不可大写——当法官漏掉某些东西时，没有什么能保证它不会再漏掉。

sentences.en: When a deterministic control point misses something, you extend the policy: that defect never passes again.
sentences.zh: 当确定性控制点遗漏某些内容时，您可以扩展策略：该缺陷永远不会再次通过。

blocks.html_en: • Context fragility — the more rules you load into a judge, the more it misses. Reliability degrades exactly as requirements accumulate.

sentences.en: Context fragility — the more rules you load into a judge, the more it misses.
sentences.zh: 上下文脆弱性——你向法官加载的规则越多，它错过的就越多。

sentences.en: Reliability degrades exactly as requirements accumulate.
sentences.zh: 可靠性随着需求的增加而降低。

sentences.en: Criterion
sentences.zh: 标准

sentences.en: LLM-judge validation
sentences.zh: LLM-法官验证

sentences.en: Deterministic harness (this project)
sentences.zh: 确定性利用（本项目）

sentences.en: Determinism
sentences.zh: 决定论

sentences.en: Token cost
sentences.zh: 代币成本

sentences.en: Per validation
sentences.zh: 每次验证

sentences.en: Near zero
sentences.zh: 接近于零

sentences.en: Partial, variable
sentences.zh: 部分、变量

sentences.en: High, reproducible
sentences.zh: 高、重现性好

sentences.en: Fixing a defect
sentences.zh: 修复缺陷

sentences.en: Not guaranteed to hold
sentences.zh: 不保证持有

sentences.en: Permanent (monotonic)
sentences.zh: 永久（单调）

sentences.en: Sensitivity to context load
sentences.zh: 对上下文负载的敏感性

sentences.en: Degrades
sentences.zh: 降级

sentences.en: Insensitive
sentences.zh: 不敏感

sentences.en: Bypass possible
sentences.zh: 可以绕过

sentences.en: Yes (model-dependent)
sentences.zh: 是（取决于型号）

sentences.en: No within the governed channel (blocking refusal)
sentences.zh: 受管制通道内否（阻止拒绝）

sentences.en: Per prompt / per project
sentences.zh: 每个提示/每个项目

sentences.en: Machine level (organization)
sentences.zh: 机器级（组织）

sentences.en: How it works
sentences.zh: 它是如何运作的

blocks.html_en: A skill here is not just a capability with a prose rule ("buttons must be blue"). It is a capability bundled with its own validation: a human-facing SKILL.md plus a machine-enforced SKILL.rego (OPA/Rego — the only supported policy format). The same policy corpus is evaluated at two moments, deterministically:

sentences.en: A skill here is not just a capability with a prose rule ("buttons must be blue").
sentences.zh: 这里的技能不仅仅是散文规则的能力（“按钮必须是蓝色的”）。

sentences.en: It is a capability bundled with its own validation: a human-facing SKILL.md plus a machine-enforced SKILL.rego (OPA/Rego — the only supported policy format).
sentences.zh: 它是一种与其自身验证捆绑在一起的功能：面向人类的 SKILL.md 加上机器强制执行的 SKILL.rego（OPA/Rego - 唯一受支持的策略格式）。

sentences.en: The same policy corpus is evaluated at two moments, deterministically:
sentences.zh: 相同的策略语料库在两个时刻进行确定性评估：

blocks.html_en: • At plan time — before anything executes, the agent's structured plan is linted (lock_in_plan). A rejected plan gets semantic violations to self-correct against; an accepted plan gets a capability ticket (an ephemeral signed token: plan fingerprint + least-privilege scope).

sentences.en: At plan time — before anything executes, the agent's structured plan is linted (lock_in_plan).
sentences.zh: 在计划时——在执行任何操作之前，代理的结构化计划会被检查（lock_in_plan）。

sentences.en: A rejected plan gets semantic violations to self-correct against; an accepted plan gets a capability ticket (an ephemeral signed token: plan fingerprint + least-privilege scope).
sentences.zh: 被拒绝的计划会出现语义违规，需要自我纠正；接受的计划将获得功能票证（临时签名令牌：计划指纹+最小权限范围）。

blocks.html_en: • At artifact time — every Edit/Write is intercepted by a control point (ppg-guard) that checks the ticket and evaluates the produced content against the same policies. A third pass (ppg-verify) re-checks the whole diff at apply/CI time.

sentences.en: At artifact time — every Edit/Write is intercepted by a control point (ppg-guard) that checks the ticket and evaluates the produced content against the same policies.
sentences.zh: 在工件时间 - 每个编辑/写入都会被控制点 (ppg-guard) 拦截，该控制点检查票证并根据相同的策略评估生成的内容。

sentences.en: A third pass (ppg-verify) re-checks the whole diff at apply/CI time.
sentences.zh: 第三遍（ppg-verify）在应用/CI 时重新检查整个差异。

blocks.html_en: No ticket, no edit. Server unreachable, no edit. The blocks are exit codes and HTTP rejections — not prompts a model can talk its way around. Enforcement is distributed (control points inside the loop, in the tools, and at apply time); the decision is centralized in one validation server, so every control point renders identical verdicts from a single policy corpus.

sentences.en: No ticket, no edit.
sentences.zh: 没有票，没有编辑。

sentences.en: Server unreachable, no edit.
sentences.zh: 服务器无法访问，无法编辑。

sentences.en: The blocks are exit codes and HTTP rejections — not prompts a model can talk its way around.
sentences.zh: 这些块是退出代码和 HTTP 拒绝——而不是提示模型可以谈论它的方式。

sentences.en: Enforcement is distributed (control points inside the loop, in the tools, and at apply time); the decision is centralized in one validation server, so every control point renders identical verdicts from a single policy corpus.
sentences.zh: 强制执行是分布式的（循环内、工具中和应用时的控制点）；该决策集中在一个验证服务器中，因此每个控制点都会从单个策略语料库中得出相同的结论。

blocks.html_en: And when rules contradict each other, the answer is: no action. A prompt cannot override a policy — the developer typing "I want them pink" gets the same refusal on every retry. When an agent livelocks against the same rejections, the server escalates deterministically (409 POLICY_CONFLICT), naming the clashing policies and appending the case to an escalation log; ppg escalations is how a human inspects the log, fixes the corpus, and closes the conflict — and once fixed, that conflict never recurs. That is the monotonic improvement loop: every escape becomes a permanent policy, not a lost prompt. (Coverage today is the livelock case; general contradiction detection is undecidable and not claimed — see the status table.)

sentences.en: And when rules contradict each other, the answer is: no action.
sentences.zh: 当规则相互矛盾时，答案是：不采取行动。

sentences.en: A prompt cannot override a policy — the developer typing "I want them pink" gets the same refusal on every retry.
sentences.zh: 提示不能覆盖策略——开发人员输入“我想要它们是粉红色的”，每次重试都会遭到同样的拒绝。

sentences.en: When an agent livelocks against the same rejections, the server escalates deterministically (409 POLICY_CONFLICT), naming the clashing policies and appending the case to an escalation log; ppg escalations is how a human inspects the log, fixes the corpus, and closes the conflict — and once fixed, that conflict never recurs.
sentences.zh: 当代理针对相同的拒绝进行活锁时，服务器会确定性地升级（409 POLICY_CONFLICT），命名冲突策略并将案例附加到升级日志中； ppg 升级是人们检查日志、修复语料库并结束冲突的方式——一旦修复，冲突就不会再发生。

sentences.en: That is the monotonic improvement loop: every escape becomes a permanent policy, not a lost prompt.
sentences.zh: 这就是单调的改进循环：每次逃脱都成为永久策略，而不是丢失的提示。

sentences.en: (Coverage today is the livelock case; general contradiction detection is undecidable and not claimed — see the status table.)
sentences.zh: （今天的报道是活锁情况；一般矛盾检测是不可判定的并且未被声明 - 请参阅状态表。）

sentences.en: Governed-machine mode (the reference mode)
sentences.zh: 受控机器模式（参考模式）

blocks.html_en: Install once per workstation; every project on the machine is governed:

sentences.en: Install once per workstation; every project on the machine is governed:
sentences.zh: 每个工作站安装一次；机器上的每个项目都受到管理：

blocks.html_en: From then on, a skill that embeds a SKILL.rego carries its enforcement with it: the harness uploads and applies it with no per-project configuration. The rule and its validation travel together — that is what makes governance capitalizable and distributable.

sentences.en: From then on, a skill that embeds a SKILL.rego carries its enforcement with it: the harness uploads and applies it with no per-project configuration.
sentences.zh: 从那时起，嵌入 SKILL.rego 的技能就会随之执行：工具会上传并应用它，而无需针对每个项目进行配置。

sentences.en: The rule and its validation travel together — that is what makes governance capitalizable and distributable.
sentences.zh: 规则及其验证相辅相成——这就是治理可资本化和可分配的原因。

blocks.html_en: Architectural invariants (ADRs) are an optional second corpus for organization-wide rules; the harness works without them (ppg -skills demo/skills starts with zero ADRs).

sentences.en: Architectural invariants (ADRs) are an optional second corpus for organization-wide rules; the harness works without them (ppg -skills demo/skills starts with zero ADRs).
sentences.zh: 架构不变量（ADR）是组织范围规则的可选第二语料库；没有它们，安全带也能工作（ppg -skills demo/skills 以零 ADR 开头）。

blocks.html_en: Then: tutorial 15 is the flagship — one skill, its own Rego, zero ADRs, and an adversarial "make the buttons pink" prompt refused deterministically. Tutorial 14 is the same demo with the org-wide corpus; 12 red-teams the whole loop, each bypass paired with its refusal or its honestly documented limit.

sentences.en: Then: tutorial 15 is the flagship — one skill, its own Rego, zero ADRs, and an adversarial "make the buttons pink" prompt refused deterministically.
sentences.zh: 然后：教程 15 是旗舰 - 一项技能，它自己的 Rego，零 ADR，以及确定性拒绝的对抗性“使按钮呈粉红色”提示。

sentences.en: Tutorial 14 is the same demo with the org-wide corpus; 12 red-teams the whole loop, each bypass paired with its refusal or its honestly documented limit.
sentences.zh: 教程 14 是使用组织范围语料库的相同演示； 12 个红队组成整个循环，每个旁路都与其拒绝或诚实记录的限制配对。

blocks.html_en: ✅ scripts with dry-run/rollback; managed setup verifies the guard binary is not user-writable

sentences.en: ✅ scripts with dry-run/rollback; managed setup verifies the guard binary is not user-writable

sentences.en: ADR-independent operation

sentences.en: Validation-server API authentication

blocks.html_en: 🟡 none by design; binds 127.0.0.1:8765 by default — a networked or multi-user deployment must front it with an auth proxy (see PoC boundaries)

sentences.en: 🟡 none by design; binds 127.0.0.1:8765 by default — a networked or multi-user deployment must front it with an auth proxy (see PoC boundaries)
sentences.zh: 🟡 没有设计；默认情况下绑定 127.0.0.1:8765 — 联网或多用户部署必须在其前面添加身份验证代理（请参阅 PoC 边界）

sentences.en: Managed-mode tamper resistance
sentences.zh: 管理模式防篡改

blocks.html_en: 🟡 managed scope closes the settings-edit vector; the guard binary (root-owned install) and PPG_* env pinning must be hardened operationally against a hostile user (see set-up-a-governed-workstation)

sentences.en: 🟡 managed scope closes the settings-edit vector; the guard binary (root-owned install) and PPG_* env pinning must be hardened operationally against a hostile user (see set-up-a-governed-workstation)
sentences.zh: 🟡 托管范围关闭设置编辑向量；防护二进制文件（root 拥有的安装）和 PPG_* env 固定必须针对敌对用户进行操作强化（请参阅设置受管理的工作站）

sentences.en: Skill validation regardless of declared skill_id
sentences.zh: 无论声明的 Skill_id 为何，技能验证

blocks.html_en: 🟡 content-view (artifact/changeset) rules always apply; a skill's plan-view ordering/workflow rule is enforced only for a plan that declares its skill_id

sentences.en: 🟡 content-view (artifact/changeset) rules always apply; a skill's plan-view ordering/workflow rule is enforced only for a plan that declares its skill_id
sentences.zh: 🟡 内容视图（工件/变更集）规则始终适用；技能的计划视图排序/工作流程规则仅针对声明其技能 ID 的计划强制执行

sentences.en: Conflict between validations → blocking escalation
sentences.zh: 验证之间的冲突 → 阻止升级

blocks.html_en: 🟡 deterministic livelock escalation: POLICY_CONFLICT after 3 rejections with an identical violation set (consecutive or not), blocking every session, surviving restarts, closed only by ppg escalations resolve; general unsatisfiability detection is undecidable and not claimed

sentences.en: 🟡 deterministic livelock escalation: POLICY_CONFLICT after 3 rejections with an identical violation set (consecutive or not), blocking every session, surviving restarts, closed only by ppg escalations resolve; general unsatisfiability detection is undecidable and not claimed
sentences.zh: 🟡 确定性活锁升级：在具有相同违规集（连续或不连续）的 3 次拒绝后出现 POLICY_CONFLICT，阻止每个会话，幸存重启，仅通过 ppg 升级解决关闭；一般不可满足性检测是不可判定的且未声明

sentences.en: Terminal/Bash writes

blocks.html_en: 🟡 out of hook reach by design; ppg-verify covers them at apply time (scripts/setup-git-backstop.sh wires it as a pre-commit hook)

sentences.en: Layout
sentences.zh: 布局

content_html_zh:
PPG——用于代理开发循环的确定性治理工具

PPG 将代理编码循环（Claude Code、GitHub Copilot）包装在确定性治理工具中：安装在机器级别的钩子、MCP 服务器和验证服务器，以便代理生成的工件可以证明遵守受治理通道内的规则 - 无需要求第二个 LLM 来监管第一个。

（该名称是一个历史缩写词——Platform Planning Gateway——保留为一个品牌；根据 ADR-130，中央组件现在称为验证服务器。）

放大代理循环和受控技能注册表（草案）的配套存储库。 词汇遵循 ADR-130；请参阅术语表。

问题

代理在 LLM 上运行，而 LLM 是不确定的。 没有什么可以保证你在上下文中设置的规则——指令、技能、设计系统——会被遵循。 更糟糕的是，当指令发生冲突时，结果就是抛硬币。 如果你的设计系统说按钮是蓝色的，而开发人员输入“不，我想要它们是粉色的”，那么你会根据模型的心情得到粉色或蓝色。 人为升级保护措施并不能解决这个问题：它们本身取决于决定升级的模型。

为什么不是LLM法官？

常见的缓解措施是第二个法学硕士审查第一个法学硕士的输出（“法学硕士作为法官”，有时称为对抗性验证）。 这有帮助，但是：

  • 成本——每次验证都会燃烧代币。
  • 信任不足——法官也不确定；它可以让相同的缺陷通过两次。
  • 不可大写——当法官漏掉某些东西时，没有什么能保证它不会再漏掉。 当确定性控制点遗漏某些内容时，您可以扩展策略：该缺陷永远不会再次通过。
  • 上下文脆弱性——你向法官加载的规则越多，它错过的就越多。 可靠性随着需求的增加而降低。
标准 LLM-法官验证 确定性利用（本项目）
决定论 No Yes
代币成本 每次验证 接近于零
Trust 部分、变量 高、重现性好
修复缺陷 不保证持有 永久（单调）
对上下文负载的敏感性 降级 不敏感
可以绕过 是（取决于型号） 受管制通道内否（阻止拒绝）
Scope 每个提示/每个项目 机器级（组织）

它是如何运作的

这里的技能不仅仅是散文规则的能力（“按钮必须是蓝色的”）。 它是一种与其自身验证捆绑在一起的功能：面向人类的 SKILL.md 加上机器强制执行的 SKILL.rego（OPA/Rego - 唯一受支持的策略格式）。 相同的策略语料库在两个时刻进行确定性评估：

  1. 在计划时——在执行任何操作之前，代理的结构化计划会被检查（lock_in_plan）。 被拒绝的计划会出现语义违规，需要自我纠正；接受的计划将获得功能票证（临时签名令牌：计划指纹+最小权限范围）。
  2. 在工件时间 - 每个编辑/写入都会被控制点 (ppg-guard) 拦截，该控制点检查票证并根据相同的策略评估生成的内容。 第三遍（ppg-verify）在应用/CI 时重新检查整个差异。

没有票，没有编辑。 服务器无法访问，无法编辑。 这些块是退出代码和 HTTP 拒绝——而不是提示模型可以谈论它的方式。 强制执行是分布式的（循环内、工具中和应用时的控制点）；该决策集中在一个验证服务器中，因此每个控制点都会从单个策略语料库中得出相同的结论。

当规则相互矛盾时，答案是：不采取行动。 提示不能覆盖策略——开发人员输入“我想要它们是粉红色的”，每次重试都会遭到同样的拒绝。 当代理针对相同的拒绝进行活锁时，服务器会确定性地升级（409 POLICY_CONFLICT），命名冲突策略并将案例附加到升级日志中； ppg 升级是人们检查日志、修复语料库并结束冲突的方式——一旦修复，冲突就不会再发生。 这就是单调的改进循环：每次逃脱都成为永久策略，而不是丢失的提示。 （今天的报道是活锁情况；一般矛盾检测是不可判定的并且未被声明 - 请参阅状态表。）

受控机器模式（参考模式）

每个工作站安装一次；机器上的每个项目都受到管理：

make install                    # binaries into ~/.local/bin
make setup-claude-code          # user-wide hooks + MCP registration
sudo make setup-claude-code-managed   # optional: root-owned, non-overridable hooks
ppg -adr examples/adr                 # the validation server (demo corpus; binds 127.0.0.1:8765)

从那时起，嵌入 SKILL.rego 的技能就会随之执行：工具会上传并应用它，而无需针对每个项目进行配置。 规则及其验证相辅相成——这就是治理可资本化和可分配的原因。

架构不变量（ADR）是组织范围规则的可选第二语料库；没有它们，安全带也能工作（ppg -skills demo/skills 以零 ADR 开头）。

Try it

make quickstart        # 1-minute guided demo

然后：教程 15 是旗舰 - 一项技能，它自己的 Rego，零 ADR，以及确定性拒绝的对抗性“使按钮呈粉红色”提示。 教程 14 是使用组织范围语料库的相同演示； 12 个红队组成整个循环，每个旁路都与其拒绝或诚实记录的限制配对。

想要先了解 30 秒的概述吗？ 观看 90 秒的动画导览：docs/diagrams/ppg-tutorials-tour.svg。

状态——已证实的内容与正在进行的内容

承诺 地位
确定性计划+工件+变更集验证（OPA/Rego，无LLM，仅限确定性内置） ✅ 实施、失败关闭、红队
能力票证、会话绑定、计划哈希固定 ✅已实施
技能与其自身的验证捆绑在一起，自动应用 ✅ MCP 路径上的项目和用户范围的 .claude/skills + .agents/skills；内容政策适用于每次编辑，无论声明的 Skill_id 为何
受管机器安装（用户+受管范围） ✅ 具有空运行/回滚功能的脚本；托管设置验证守卫二进制文件不可由用户写入
独立于 ADR 的运营 ✅ 验证服务器以 -skills 开头，没有 ADR 语料库
验证-服务器API认证 🟡 没有设计；默认情况下绑定 127.0.0.1:8765 — 联网或多用户部署必须在其前面添加身份验证代理（请参阅 PoC 边界）
管理模式防篡改 🟡 托管范围关闭设置编辑向量；防护二进制文件（root 拥有的安装）和 PPG_* env 固定必须针对敌对用户进行操作强化（请参阅设置受管理的工作站）
无论声明的 Skill_id 为何，技能验证 🟡 内容视图（工件/变更集）规则始终适用；技能的计划视图排序/工作流程规则仅针对声明其技能 ID 的计划强制执行
验证之间的冲突 → 阻止升级 🟡 确定性活锁升级：在具有相同违规集（连续或不连续）的 3 次拒绝后出现 POLICY_CONFLICT，阻止每个会话，幸存重启，仅通过 ppg 升级解决关闭；一般不可满足性检测是不可判定的且未声明
终端/Bash 写入 🟡 设计上超出了钩子的范围； ppg-verify 在应用时覆盖它们（scripts/setup-git-backstop.sh 将其连接为预提交挂钩）

AUDIT.md 按声明跟踪一致性声明。 已知的 PoC 边界：基于关键字的检索、模拟沙箱、对称的每台机器票据密钥、未经身份验证的 API（绑定到本地主机）。

你想要… Read
运行它并看到拒绝发生 文档/教程/
安装受管理的工作站 文档/操作方法/set-up-a-governed-workstation.md

布局

cmd/ppg/                 validation server (enrich, lock_in_plan, tools, verify_artifact, verify_changeset, debt_report, validate_skill, register_skill)
cmd/ppg-verify/          apply-time / CI control point: verifies the working-tree diff via /verify_changeset
cmd/svc-mock/            local stand-in for a cataloged service (runs the discovery tutorial out-of-the-box)
internal/adr/            ADR store loading + invariant retrieval
internal/enrich/         amplifier context builder
internal/catalog/        service catalog store + Rego-backed ranking (discovery)
internal/plan/           structured plan contract (see schemas/plan.schema.json)
internal/linter/         OPA/Rego plan linter, policies tagged amplifier|compensatory
internal/ticket/         capability ticket (JWT: plan_hash + scope, session-bound + configurable TTL)
internal/smarttools/     ticket guard + sandbox + semantic analyzers
internal/skill/          skill parsing + OPA/Rego governance linter + security tiers
internal/debt/           transition-debt report
internal/store/          per-machine ticket/session storage (TokenStore/SessionStore, see ADR-100)
internal/auth/           demo fixture only — frozen-legacy target for ADR-070 tutorials, not product code
internal/payment/        demo fixture only — payment router edited in the tutorials, not product code
examples/                fictional demo corpus — replace with your own (see examples/README.md)
skill-governance/        skill governance policies (structure.rego, security.rego)
schemas/                 language-neutral JSON Schema of the plan contract
adapters/preflight/      black-box adapter (writes .cursorrules / copilot-instructions.md)
adapters/claudecode/     Claude Code adapter: MCP server (planning) + PreToolUse control point
adapters/copilot/        GitHub Copilot adapter: PreToolUse control point (ppg-copilot-guard)
scripts/                 setup/remove scripts for the governed workstation
Makefile                 build, install, and setup/remove targets
demo/                    APM package: three skills (ppg-tutorial, add-payment-method, design-system)
docs/                    Diátaxis documentation + decisions (ADR-130) + PlantUML diagrams
